commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Unlike. The wise, or themselves. Hebrew and Septuagint, "inconstant."

commentaries.author: Gregory The Dialogist
commentaries.content: The heart of the wise is always consistent, because, while it remains at peace in its upright convictions, it constantly urges itself to good deeds. But the heart of the fool is inconsistent, because, in exhibiting itself as variable and changeable, it never remains what it was. .
